codeReview.summary: The fix commit resolves 5 of the 6 previously blocking issues (DateTime urgency semantics, drift empty-iterator handling, oscillation horizon=None regression safety, compensating-strategy month/year scaling direction, and children_by_horizon Vec API). One prior blocking issue remains: panic paths via `.expect()` in public Horizon range methods are still reachable through publicly constructible enum variants.

issues.description: `range_start()`/`range_end()` still use `.expect(...)` in public paths (e.g., lines 164, 169, 173, 197, 209, 216). The new comments claim invalid states are unreachable, but `Horizon` variants remain publicly constructible (`pub enum Horizon` at line 39), so malformed values like `Horizon::Month(2026, 13)` can still panic at runtime. This does not satisfy the requested fix to remove panic paths or make them unreachable by design.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill still appears to be marked followed without recorded Manual Verification evidence (notably `cargo doc -p sd-core --no-deps`).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill requires this in .factory/skills/rust-library-worker/SKILL.md lines 68-75; fix handoff reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` with only `verification.commandsRun` listed and no manual/interactive verification block (/Users/moritzbierling/.factory/missions/30fe7d21-09b3-49fb-8f42-d5d2ffc0b72b/handoffs/2026-03-08T03-19-08-217Z__fix-scrutiny-blocking-issues__e8ffec91-70bd-4045-a7e0-f2c404e011a2.json lines 14-43).

summary: FAIL: this re-run clears most prior blockers, but the panic-path safety blocker in horizon range methods remains unresolved because `.expect()` is still reachable from publicly constructible Horizon variants.
